claim_gate.reason: Exact finite character moments transfer non-scalar bounded-support action to the audited typical blocks, and mixed moments certify independent traceless directions in many blocks, but they do not control noncommutation, all-n eigenvalue collisions, minimum gaps, circuit synthesis, or decoding.

falsifiers_triggered: One transposition/3-cycle orbit average is scalar on two audited n=8 multiplicity blocks.
Adding a second bounded-support observable repairs finite non-scalarity but does not prove simple joint spectrum.
A positive centered covariance rank proves linear independence, not noncommutation or algebra generation.
Positive finite variance supplies no lower bound on the minimum eigenvalue gap.
Factorial exact character enumeration is not an efficient quantum or classical implementation.
No outcome-information or hidden-involution decoding theorem is implied by multiplicity-space non-scalarity.
summary: Exact character moments audited 32 nontrivial typical multiplicity blocks at n=(7, 8). The bounded-support portfolio is non-scalar on 32 and scalar on 0; 29 have at least two independent traceless directions and 3 additional two-dimensional blocks have finite simple spectrum. No uniform gap, transform, or decoder follows.

theorem_contract.decomposition: A_O commutes with diagonal S_n and acts as I_nu tensor M_nu on each nu multiplicity block.
theorem_contract.diameter_consequence: Popoviciu's inequality gives spectral diameter at least 2*sqrt(variance).
theorem_contract.enumeration_cap: The current exact permutation contraction is capped at n=8. Extending it requires a class-algebra formula, not factorial enumeration.
theorem_contract.non_scalar_certificate: Tr(M_nu^2)/g_nu-(Tr(M_nu)/g_nu)^2>0 exactly iff the finite Hermitian block is non-scalar.
theorem_contract.second_moment_identity: Tr(M_nu^2)=(|O||S_n|)^-1 sum_y sum_g chi_nu(g) chi_lambda(g a a_y) chi_mu(g b b_y).
